title: FINRA Case 23-02561 — Catherine Ellis v. Morgan Stanley
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 23-02561
Catherine A. Ellis
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Orlando, Florida
Morgan Stanley
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Customer vs. Member
This case was decided by an all-public panel.
The evidentiary hearing was conducted partially by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
For Claimant Catherine A. Ellis (“Claimant”): Michael C. Bixby, Esq., Bixby Law PLLC,
Pensacola, Florida.
For Respondent Morgan Stanley (“Respondent”): Kendra S. Canape, Esq. and Rachel
Weitzman, Esq., Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP, Irvine, California.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: September 20, 2023.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: September 20, 2023.
Statement of Answer filed on or about: December 1, 2023.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: December 1, 2023.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted the following causes of action: violations of the
Securities Act and the Blue Sky Acts and securities laws; breach of fiduciary duty; violation of
FINRA/NYSE/SEC rules and regulations, breach of contract and negligence; and negligent
supervision.The causes of action related to Claimant’s allegation that Respondent mishandled
her assets by giving improper advice, failing to give prudent, proper advice, failing to correct
their error, recommending an inappropriate investment strategy, and putting Respondent’s

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02561
Award Page 2 of 5
interests ahead of Claimant. Claimant further alleged that Respondent sold Claimant a variety of
high risk “Private Equity” or illiquid non-conventional investments, such as CPG Vintage Access
Fund II, Skybridge Mul-AD Ser. G, Blackstone REIT, exposing her to unnecessary risks and
causing losses and underperformance.
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer, Respondent denied the allegations made
in the Statement of Claim and asserted various affirmative defenses.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant requested:
(a) Actual damages of no less than $750,000.00, as well as damages for the loss of income that
would have been received had Claimant’s money been managed properly, as well as all other
losses, foreseeable or not, that Claimant suffered, including non-pecuniary losses including
emotional distress damages;
(b) Disgorgement and return of all fees, management charges, and commissions;
(c) Interest on Claimant’s losses at the legal rate;
(d) Claimant’s costs, legal fees and expenses;
(e) Rescission and/or statutory damages;
(f) Punitive damages; and
(e) Such other and additional damages and relief as may be shown at hearing and which the
Panel deems just and equitable.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent requested that the Panel (1) dismiss all claims in the
Statement of Claim in their entirety; (2) expungement on behalf of Unnamed Person James Eric
Monken (“Monken”) of all references to Occurrence Number 2297626 from Central Registration
Depository (“CRD”) registration records (CRD Number 5765921); and (3) award Respondent
costs in defense of this proceeding.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrators acknowledge that they have each read the pleadings and other materials filed by
the parties.
The parties had the opportunity to present oral argument and evidence on the request for
expungement on behalf of Monken at the evidentiary hearing. Monken and Respondent’s
representative appeared by videoconference at the evidentiary hearing. Claimant appeared by
videoconference and opposed the request for expungement.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the hearing, and any
post-hearing submissions, the Panel has decided in full and final resolution of the issues
submitted for determination as follows:
1. Respondent is liable for and shall pay to Claimant the sum of $225,000.00 in compensatory
damages.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02561
Award Page 3 of 5
2. Respondent’s request for expungement on behalf of Unnamed Person James Eric Monken of
Occurrence Number 2297626 from registration records maintained by the CRD is denied with
prejudice.
3. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein, including any requests for
punitive damages, treble damages, and attorneys’ fees, are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code of Arbitration Procedure, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 1,740.00
Expungement Filing Fee =$ 1,600.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 2,625.00
Member Process Fee =$ 5,225.00
Late Pre-Hearing Cancellation Fees
Fees apply when a pre-hearing conference is cancelled within three business days of the
scheduled conference:
August 30, 2024, cancellation requested by the parties =$ 100.00
Total Late Pre-Hearing Cancellation Fees =$ 100.00
The Panel has assessed $50.00 of the late pre-hearing cancellation fees to Claimant.
The Panel has assessed $50.00 of the late pre-hearing cancellation fees to Respondent.
Postponement Fees
Postponements granted during these proceedings for which fees were assessed or waived:
November 24-25, 2025, postponement requested by the parties =$ 1,325.00
Total Postponement Fees =$ 1,325.00
The Panel has assessed $662.50 of the postponement fees to Claimant.
The Panel has assessed $662.50 of the postponement fees to Respondent.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02561
Award Page 4 of 5
Last-Minute Cancellation Fees
Fees apply when a hearing on the merits is cancelled within ten calendar days before the start
of a scheduled hearing session:
November 24-25, 2025, cancellation requested by the parties =$ 1,800.00
Total Last-Minute Cancellation Fees =$ 1,800.00
The Panel has assessed $900.00 of the last-minute cancellation fees to Claimant.
The Panel has assessed $900.00 of the last-minute cancellation fees to Respondent.
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Panel has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator(s), including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator(s), which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
One (1) pre-hearing session with a single Arbitrator @ $450.00/session
Pre-Hearing Conference: November 12, 2024 1 session =$ 450.00
One (1) pre-hearing session with the Panel @ $1,325.00/session
Pre-Hearing Conference: January 29, 2024 1 session =$ 1,325.00
Twenty-four (24) hearing sessions @ $1,325.00/session =$ 31,800.00
Hearings: May 12, 2025 2 sessions
May 13, 2025 2 sessions
May 14, 2025 2 sessions
May 15, 2025 2 sessions
May 16, 2025 2 sessions
September 2, 2025 2 sessions
September 3, 2025 3 sessions
September 4, 2025 3 sessions
September 5, 2025 2 sessions
February 17, 2026 2 sessions
February 23, 2026 2 sessions
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 33,575.00
The Panel has assessed $16,787.50 of the hearing session fees to Claimant.
The Panel has assessed $16,787.50 of the hearing session fees to Respondent.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 23-02561
Award Page 5 of 5
ARBITRATION PANEL
Christopher J. Klein - Public Arbitrator, Presiding Chairperson
Anthony Richard Sanchez, III - Public Arbitrator
Lloyd Green - Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
